name: Secure Agent Mesh — Harshit Kumar
description: A two-agent A2A mesh for querying Life Progress Indicators via the SMILE methodology. Agent A (client, port 5001) validates and routes user queries; Agent B (server, port 5002) executes LPI tools (smile_overview, query_knowledge) and returns structured answers with provenance. Includes prompt-injection defence, rate limiting, subprocess timeout, and output whitelisting.

supportedInterfaces.comment: POST { query: string } — entry-point for Agent A. Requires Ollama running on localhost:11434.
POST { query: string, from_agent: 'agent_a' } — Agent B specialist endpoint.

skills.name: SMILE Overview
skills.description: Accepts a natural-language question about the SMILE framework and returns a structured overview covering Social, Meaning, Interests, Livelihoods, and Environment dimensions with associated LPI indicators. Input: free-text query (max 512 chars). Output: JSON with dimension, summary, lpi_indicators.
examples: What does the Social dimension of SMILE measure?
Give me an overview of the SMILE framework for urban communities.

skills.name: LPI Knowledge Query
skills.description: Queries the LPI knowledge base for detailed indicator data. Returns answer, sources, and a confidence score. Input: free-text query (max 512 chars). Output: JSON with answer, sources, confidence.
examples: Which LPI indicators are most relevant to financial resilience?
How does the LPI score housing security?

authentication.comment: Localhost-only deployment. Caller identity is verified via the from_agent field.

_lpiMetadata.explainability: Every response includes a provenance array. Each entry records the tool name, the exact args passed, a sanitised response snippet (first 400 chars), elapsed time, and the model used. This gives a complete call chain from user query to final answer.
